creator: mva@FreeBSD.org
text:
On, Fri Oct 11, 2013, Thierry Thomas wrote:

> Le jeu 10 oct 13 Ã  18:53:48 +0200, mva@FreeBSD.org <mva@FreeBSD.org>
>  Ã©crivaitÂ :
> > Synopsis: print/pslib: fix includedir in libps.pc
> >
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> > State-Changed-By: mva
> > State-Changed-When: Thu Oct 10 16:53:48 UTC 2013
> > State-Changed-Why:
> > By default the pslib examples use a subdirectory below ${PREFIX}/include
> > to find the correct header files. Do you have any ports or applications
> > to use as show case for that issue and which work flawlessly on other
> > platforms?
>
> I noticed that when updating the port science/gnudatalanguage (just
> committed). Its CMake module is smart enough to find pslib, but
> anyway, it would be safer to install a correct libps.pc.

Correct in which term? It works that way (reporting ${PREFIX}/include
through pkg-config) in the upstream package and on every other
platform. The examples and documentation use libps/pslib.h as include
directive, not pslib.h. Thus, we can consider it being the intention of
the upstream author (as it is for X11 - see the output of 'pkg-config
--cflags x11').

Unless there is a very good reason to change it (e.g. a majority of
other projects  expecting it to work differently), I rather would not
want to move away from the provided default, since the FreeBSD port
would be the only one behaving different from the rest.

Cheers
Marcus
